meta.name: Soul Food Candied Yams
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Baked sweet potatoes in a buttery brown-sugar syrup with warm spices. Marshmallow topping is optional.

meta.summary: Tender baked sweet potatoes coated in a glossy brown-sugar butter syrup with cinnamon and warm spice—classic holiday soul food.

notes: Sweet potatoes are done when very tender; internal temps often exceed 200°F.
Avoid scorching sugars by baking covered first, then uncovering to glaze.

ingredients.label: Sweet potatoes (often called yams), peeled and sliced 1/2-inch thick

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep dish
steps.body: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly butter a 9x13-inch baking dish.

steps.title: Slice sweet potatoes
steps.body: Peel sweet potatoes and slice into 1/2-inch rounds. Arrange in the baking dish in overlapping layers.

steps.title: Make the candied syrup
steps.body: In a saucepan over medium heat, melt butter. Stir in brown sugar, granulated sugar, water (or orange juice), cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger (optional), and salt. Bring to a gentle simmer 2–3 minutes until glossy. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla (and lemon juice if using).

steps.title: Pour syrup and cover
steps.body: Pour syrup evenly over sweet potatoes. Cover tightly with foil and bake 35–45 minutes, until potatoes begin to soften.

steps.title: Uncover and baste
steps.body: Remove foil. Spoon/baste syrup over potatoes. Continue baking 15–25 minutes uncovered, basting once or twice, until potatoes are very tender and syrup thickens into a glaze.

steps.title: Optional: add marshmallows
steps.body: If using marshmallows, sprinkle on top during the final 8–10 minutes of baking, until melted and lightly browned.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.body: Rest 10 minutes so glaze thickens further. Serve warm with the syrup spooned over each portion.
